rulings.Case Title: KIERAN BUCKLEY et al VS. DAVID BARTENWERFER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defts To Reopen Discovery For Limted Purposes; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 3, 2012, Line 12, CROSS COMPLAINANTS DAVID BARTENWERFER, KATE BARTENWERFER'S Motion To Reopen Discovery For Limted Purposes AND Request For Judicial Notice. Denied. Defendants have not exercised diligence in moving to reopen discovery approximately six months after the discovery cutoff from the original trial date of February 27, 2012 (CCP § 2024.050.) Defendants' request for judicial notice is unopposed, and therefore granted. Defendants' supplemental request for judicial notice filed in support of their reply is improper, and therefore denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: SIMEON J OSBORN VS. ALEXANDER PRESNIAKOV
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Private Place Order Against Judgment Debtor To Satisfy Money Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 3, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SIMEON OSBORN's Motion For Private Place Order Against Judgment Debtor To Satisfy Money Judgment. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GUY H MOORE JR VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg Memo In Supp Of Mtn For Writ Of Admin Mandamus;Index; Req For Judicial Notice; Nc Of Lodging
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 3, 2012, Line 3, PETITIONER GUY MOORE's motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamusand request For Judicial Notice. Denied as to the petition and as to request for judicial notice. The court exercises its independent judgment on the evidentiary record of the administrative proceedings. (Evenson v. Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (1976) 62 Cal.App.3d 1005, 1014 -1015.) The court finds that Respondent's decision is supported by the weight of the evidence. Resigning based on the anticipation of one's employment being terminated does not establish good cause. (Unemp. Ins. Code § 1256; Morgan v. CUIAB (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 762, 767-768.) Based on the totality of the circumstances, Petitioner did not have good cause to resign. Petitioner consented to random drug tests as a condition of employment. (Admin. Record., p. 00027:19-21.) Petitioner testified that his employer did not state that he would be terminated for a deemed refusal of a drug test. (Id. at p. 00022:26 - 00023:3, 00024:13-21.) Further, Petitioner tendered his resignation approximately one half hour prior to the end of the two hour period under a mistaken belief that he would be fired, if he did not produce a sample immediately at the conclusion of the two hour period. (Id. at p. 00025:15-16.) Indeed, it does not appear that Petitioner would have any better a case if it had been established that his employer had a policy of deeming a two hours delay to be a refusal, and to terminate an employee who was so deemed to have refused to provide a specimen. The arguments regarding medical condition, race and the economy that are first raised in this petition are not part of the administrative record, and so are not considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS MOLLOY VS. BLUE SHIELD, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield'S Motion And Change In Date Of Hearing On Mtn To Protect Third Party Witness Dr. Mary Scheltheis And Other Witnesses From Harassment Or Intimidation By Plaintiff; Req. For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5650.00 Against Dennis Malloy
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 3, 2012, Line 10, Defendant California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield'S Motion To Protect Third Party Witness Dr. Mary Scheltheis And Other Witnesses From Harassment Or Intimidation By Plaintiff; ReqUEST For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5650.00 Against Dennis Malloy. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Cohen modified as follows: Denied. The evidence submitted in support of the Motion does not show that Mr. Molloy tampered with or intimidated the witness. At the hearing on July 26, 2012 counsel for Blue Shield confirmed that Molloy's sole direct communication with the doctor (as opposed to her office staff) was the letter-Exhibit E. This does not suggest witness tampering. In part of the deposition testimony not highlighted by the moving party, the Doctor was asked if the letter from Molloy at issue here was intimidating. Her answer: "Not after reading it again. I mean, it's -no." Ex. F, Depo. Transcript, 157:18-19. For this among other reasons, there is no merit to the motion. Plaintiff did not cite any law or provide any competent evidence to support his request for sanctions. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
